title: Understanding Absolute References
text: When you copy a formula, Excel automatically adjusts the cell references. To prevent this, you need to use absolute references. Absolute references keep the cell reference constant, no matter where you copy the formula.

title: Identifying Absolute References
text: Absolute references are indicated by a dollar sign ($) before the column letter and row number. For example, $A$1 is an absolute reference to cell A1.

title: Applying Absolute References
text: To keep the formula in cell H30 constant when copying, you need to change the formula from $$=H1+H8$$ to use absolute references. What would the formula look like with absolute references?
